specific_mechanism: CHEEGER-Attn: λ_2-monitored attention bottleneck regularizer. For each attention head h at layer l, symmetrize attention map: Ã_{l,h} = (A_{l,h} + A_{l,h}^T)/2. Compute Laplacian L = D - Ã. Estimate λ_2 via power iteration. Add regularizer R = Σ -log(λ_2(L_{l,h}) + ε) to training loss to push spectral gap up; bottlenecks → high regularizer penalty.
llm_application: CHEEGER-Attn: each forward pass computes per-head per-layer λ_2 of symmetrized attention Laplacian via 5-step power iteration. Add R = - Σ_{l,h} log(λ_2 + ε) × λ_C as auxiliary training-time loss. By Cheeger inequality, high λ_2 implies high attention conductance — no narrow bottleneck. The regularizer pushes attention patterns to be well-mixed across tokens.
novelty_claim: SpectralGap (arXiv 2505.15177) uses Laplacian gaps for graph OOD detection; Spectral Attention Networks (openreview) use Laplacian PE. Cheeger-Attn applies the Cheeger bound DIRECTLY as a per-head attention regularizer — different framing.
motivation_justification: Cheeger inequality is a rigorous algebraic bound on the spectral gap. The candidate transfers the bound directly to attention-graph Laplacian as bottleneck signal.
sub_mechanisms: M_2: Graph Laplacian L = D - Ã
M_3: λ_2 estimation via power iteration
